decisions.summary: Foundation already landed in commit e9914c9429 (record schema superset, src/lib/pan-dir/record.ts writer, sync-path status-override writer/reader, harness/model mirror, no-loss test — 63 related tests green). This plan scopes ONLY the remaining gaps + verification; the work agent verifies-and-completes, does not rebuild the foundation.

Record name = 'record' at <workspace>/.pan/records/<issue>.json on the feature branch (already implemented). Unifies with the PAN-1908 records plane; retires the 'continues' name the maintainer disliked.

Pipeline verdicts: keep the existing 'pipeline' mirror field (PanIssuePipelineRecord, from PAN-1908) in the record but do NOT expand verdict handling here. Making verdicts reconstruction-safe is PAN-1922. This issue keeps the record about WORK progress.
Backfill targets only IN-FLIGHT issues (live workspace/branch). The 132 historical closed .pan/continues/ files remain read-only legacy and are NOT migrated — resume only matters for in-flight work.

Continues become READ-ONLY LEGACY, not mass-deleted (AC6's 'removed or read-only legacy' escape hatch). No new writes to workspace continue.json or project .pan/continues/; readers stay for legacy/backfill.
CI guard and the enforced single-write-surface API are PAN-1921, NOT this issue. Here we only stop legacy writes far enough to make continues read-only, plus a one-shot no-loss assertion test.
No pan_records entry added to projects.yaml — the record is branch-resident and resolves from the workspace path; backfill's leftover infra-repo coupling is reconciled to the branch path.

hazards.summary: Split-brain already shipped: sync CLI path writes the record (vbrief/io.ts), but the async dashboard path vbrief/dag.ts:867-929 (mirrorTaskOperationToContinueFile) still writes workspace continue.json. Status overrides disagree depending on which path ran.
hazards.mitigation: Fix dag.ts FIRST (bead fix-async-statusoverride-splitbrain) so both paths converge on writeStatusOverride. No fallback chains — root cause.

hazards.summary: Migrating planning-spawn persistence (spawn-planning-session.ts:598) changes the exact path that wrote THIS session's continue.json.
hazards.mitigation: After the change, verify a planning session still persists/resumes its sessionHistory via the record.

hazards.summary: Backfill data loss: a wrong field map in records.ts permanently loses state once continues are retired.
hazards.mitigation: Bead complete-backfill-migration is inspection-gated (requiresInspection); retire-continues bead is blocked on it. Backfill test asserts statusOverrides+harness+model+schemaVersion:2 land.

hazards.summary: The record file lands on main at branch merge (it is git-tracked, not ignored).
hazards.mitigation: Acceptable — continues already land on main today. Keep record writes on the branch only during work; never commit the record to main mid-work (PAN-1124).

hazards.summary: getIssueRecordPath reconstructs the workspace path from project root + workspaces/feature-<id>; container/remote contexts may differ from the live cwd.
hazards.mitigation: Prefer getIssueRecordPathForWorkspace(workspacePath, issueId) where an explicit workspace path is available; note in the dashboard-route bead.

sessionHistory.content: Planning session for PAN-1919 (--auto). Discovered a plan-after-code situation: commit e9914c9429 already landed the record foundation (schema superset, writer, sync-path wiring, harness/model mirror, no-loss test). No existing spec/beads. Scoped a remaining-work plan of 10 beads: fix async split-brain, route 4 continue-writer surfaces to the record, complete the backfill (inspection-gated), retire continues to read-only legacy, plus no-loss-guard + cross-machine-resume + legacy-test beads.

## REQUIRED: Fix the failing check, push, and request a new review

1. Read the error output above carefully
2. Fix the code causing the failure
3. Run the failing check locally to verify it passes
4. Commit every change
5. Invoke the /rebase-and-submit skill for PAN-1919 — this is an atomic task. Because verification already ran once (a PR exists), the skill will push your branch and run `pan review request PAN-1919 -m "Fixed test"` for you. NEVER curl `/api/review/...` or any dashboard endpoint — `pan review request` is the only supported re-entry point.

Do NOT stop between steps. Do NOT stop after pushing. Do NOT stop until `pan review request` has completed successfully.
